bookings.title: Campervan Reykjavik · Iceland road-trip rental
bookings.notes: Pickup/return dates, vehicle and price are in the email's booking.pdf — start/end shown here are the Iceland trip span as placeholders; open the email to confirm exact times.

bookings.title: Camping Lido Luzern · Lucerne
bookings.notes: Camping Lido Luzern, Lidostrasse 19 (lakeside motorhome pitch ~CHF 52–68/night for 2 + van in Aug; 2.5 km from old town, next to the Lido swim; book ~2 mths ahead — fills fast on Aug weekends)

bookings.title: Camping Jungfrau · Lauterbrunnen
bookings.notes: Camping Jungfrau (Hobby 3), Lauterbrunnen (valley-floor van pitch facing Staubbach Falls ~CHF 56–72/night for 2 + van in Aug; walk to village + Wengen/Mürren lifts; book 2–3 mths ahead — sells out for peak Aug. Cheaper value option: Camping Schützenbach ~CHF 40–52/night, 1 km south). Nights 1 & 2 (3–4 Aug) — same valley base, no teardown between Lauterbrunnen and Jungfraujoch days.

bookings.title: Camping Eigernordwand · Grindelwald
bookings.notes: Camping Eigernordwand, Grindelwald-Grund (valley-floor van pitch right under the Eiger north face ~CHF 50–66/night for 2 + van in Aug; 600 m walk to the Grindelwald Terminal for the First gondola & Eiger Express; book 2–3 mths ahead — peak-Aug fills. Value swap: Camping Gletscherdorf ~CHF 44–58/night, smaller & quieter, 1.2 km east of the village)

bookings.title: Camping Attermenzen · Randa
bookings.notes: Camping Attermenzen, Randa (the working gateway pitch for car-free Zermatt, between the Randa & Täsch stations ~CHF 33–46/night for 2 + van in Aug, renovated 2021 with ~320 power sockets & a proper motorhome service point; trains run into Zermatt every ~20 min from Täsch/Randa, or the campsite shuttle-taxi ~CHF 7 each way; book 2–3 mths ahead for peak Aug). Note: the old Camping Alphubel in Täsch is permanently closed (rockfall/flood hazard), so Attermenzen is the realistic van base — leave the van here two nights (6–7 Aug) and day-trip Zermatt/Gornergrat. Cheaper swap if full: Camping Mischabel in Saas-Grund ~CHF 30–42/night, ~40 min drive.

bookings.title: Camping Innsbruck Kranebitten · Innsbruck
bookings.notes: Camping Innsbruck Kranebitten, Kranebitter Allee 214 (the city's only proper campsite — riverside van pitches on the Inn under the Nordkette, ~€36–46/night for 2 + van in Aug; bus O/J ~15 min into the old town, on-site bar/restaurant; book ~1–2 mths ahead. Value/quiet swap: Stellplatz Völs or Camping Natterer See ~7 km SW ~€40–52). Nights 1 & 2 (8–9 Aug) — van stays put for the Nordkette day.

bookings.title: Camping Nord-Sam · Salzburg
bookings.notes: Camping Nord-Sam, Salzburg (Samstrasse 22A, NE edge of the city ~€38–50/night for 2 + van in Aug; small friendly site with a pool, bus line 6/21 ~20 min to the old town & Mirabell, easy A1 roll-in after Innsbruck; book ~1 mth ahead — Aug fills on weekends). Value/quiet swap: Panorama Camping Stadtblick on the Rauchenbichl hill ~€36–48/night with a postcard fortress view, or the cheaper bare Salzburg city Stellplatz (no facilities, walk to the bus).

bookings.title: Camping am See · Obertraun
bookings.notes: Camping am See, Obertraun (Winkl 77, directly on Lake Hallstatt's quiet south shore ~€34–46/night for 2 + van in Aug; own swim beach, and the Dachstein/Krippenstein Five Fingers cable car is ~5 min away — far better value than squeezing into tiny Hallstatt itself). Take the ~10 min ferry or the lakeside cycle path into Hallstatt village early, before the day-trippers. Book ~1–1.5 mths ahead for peak Aug. Value swap: Camping Klausner-Höll right in Hallstatt is closer but cramped & pricier (~€44–56); Bad Goisern's Camping Lambichl ~€32–42 is the cheapest base if both lakefronts are full.

bookings.title: Seecamp Zell am See · Zell am See

location.name: Seecamp Zell am See, Zell am See

bookings.notes: Seecamp Zell am See, Thumersbach (Thumersbacher Strasse 34, right on the lake's east shore ~€42–56/night for 2 + van in Aug; own swim jetty + restaurant, and a sunset view back across the water to the town and the Kitzsteinhorn glacier). You arrive after running the Grossglockner High Alpine Road (toll ~€40/car, buy online & it's valid all day), so a lakefront pitch is the payoff. Book 1–2 mths ahead — peak Aug fills. Value swap: Sportcamp Woferlgut in Bruck ~10 min west ~€38–50/night (huge wellness site, great in rain), or the cheaper Panorama Camp Zell ~€34–44 on the hillside.

bookings.title: Camping Olympia · Toblach
bookings.notes: Camping Olympia, Toblach/Dobbiaco (Via Major 28, a big well-run year-round site on the valley floor ~€40–54/night for 2 + van in Aug; pool, sauna, on-site shop/restaurant and a direct cycle path to Lago di Dobbiaco — an ideal first Dolomites base after the long drive from Zell am See). Bus 442/445 and the Dolomiti SuperSummer mobility card put Tre Cime and Cortina within reach without moving the van. Book 1–2 mths ahead for peak Aug. Value swap: Camping Toblacher See ~€36–46 right on the little lake, or Niederdorf's Stellplatz/agriturismo pitches if Olympia is full.

bookings.title: Camping Olympic · Cortina d'Ampezzo
bookings.notes: Camping Olympic, Cortina d'Ampezzo (Località Fiames, on the SS51 ~4 km N of Cortina ~€44–58/night for 2 + van in Aug; pine-shaded riverside pitches, bus stop at the gate into Cortina, and you're already pointed at Misurina/Tre Cime for an early start before the Lago di Braies vehicle ban kicks in at 9:30). Book 1–2 mths ahead for peak Aug. Value swap: Camping Cortina (Località Campo) slightly closer to town ~€46–60, or the cheaper lakeside pitches up at Misurina if you want to wake under the Sorapis cliffs.

bookings.title: Camping Marmotta · Pozza di Fassa
bookings.notes: Camping Marmotta, Pozza di Fassa (Strada de Sèn Jan, in Val di Fassa ~30 min S of Ortisei over the passes ~€38–52/night for 2 + van in Aug; quiet riverside pitches, pool, on the bus line for the Catinaccio/Vajolet lifts — a great-value Dolomites base on the far side of the Sella loop you drive today). Book 1–2 mths ahead for peak Aug. Value swaps: Camping Vidor in Pozza (large, full facilities) ~€40–54, or — if you'd rather end in the city — Camping Moosbauer in Bolzano ~€42–56 with a direct bus to the centre and an easy A22 launch to Venice next morning.

bookings.title: Camping Jolly delle Querce · Mestre
bookings.notes: Camping Jolly delle Querce, Marghera/Mestre (Via Giuseppe de Marchi 7, the closest proper campsite to the Indie Campers Venice depot ~€36–50/night for 2 + van in Aug; pool + restaurant, ACTV bus into Piazzale Roma ~15–20 min for a last lagoon evening). This is your calm pre-hand-back night — dump the grey/black tanks, refill water, and clean out the van here so the 11:00 return next morning is stress-free. Book ~1 mth ahead. Value swap: Camping Venezia (Marghera) or the cheaper Camping Fusina on the lagoon edge ~€34–46 with its own vaporetto to Venice — pick by which is nearer your specific depot.
